featureName: BUG-003: docs/Development.md inventory drift with no freshness guard

completedPhaseClaims.summary: DevOps probe of spec 032 managed docs vs on-disk reality: found internal/scopesdriftguard/ (added 2026-06-05) absent from docs/Development.md Go Packages table (33 dirs on disk vs 32 documented) and no mechanical freshness guard (the design's deferred Risk #1 mitigation). Migrations (38) and prompt contracts (21) verified already fresh.

Decisions D-001..D-006: remediate the concrete drift and deliver the deferred freshness control as a Go contract test (mirrors internal/scopesdriftguard + internal/deploy compose contract test) rather than a new CLI subcommand or CI-workflow edit — runs under ./smackerel.sh test unit --go and CI with zero new surface. Fail-loud, no defaults.

Single scope: document internal/scopesdriftguard/ + internal/docfreshness/ in docs/Development.md and add internal/docfreshness/doc_freshness_test.go. Three scenarios SCN-032-D12/D13/D14 mapped 1:1 to T-DOCFRESH-001/002/003 with the adversarial anti-tautology case as regression.

Authored internal/docfreshness/doc_freshness_test.go (test-only package, runtime.Caller(0) repo-root pattern, recursive Go-source package detection matching the spec 032 find-probe). Added the two missing Go Packages rows to docs/Development.md. Fixed a transient gopls-injected duplicate package clause; gofmt-canonical via ./smackerel.sh format.

Red->green: BEFORE the doc rows the guard reported '34 packages on disk, 2 undocumented: docfreshness, scopesdriftguard' and FAILED; AFTER the rows it reported '34/0, 38/0, 21/0' and passed (ok internal/docfreshness). Captured in report.md Before/After.

Adversarial anti-tautology test TestDocFreshness_AdversarialUndocumentedItemsDetected builds a synthetic repo, asserts each scan reports exactly its synthetic missing item, and asserts the green path passes once documented. PASS. Persistent under go test ./...
F1 (drift) and F2 (missing automation) both closed one-to-one. Guard enforces all three inventories under the Go unit suite + CI; ./smackerel.sh test unit --go --go-run TestDocFreshness exit 0.

./smackerel.sh format --check exit 0 (gofmt clean) and ./smackerel.sh lint exit 0 (go vet ./... clean, ruff All checks passed!, web validation passed). Only changed Go file: internal/docfreshness/doc_freshness_test.go.

Chaos surface is the adversarial synthetic-repo perturbation in the guard test, which would re-detect any neutering of the freshness checks. Verified zero runtime/source/config/CI files modified via git status.
No simplification opportunity — the guard is one ~230-line test file with pure shared helpers (scanInternalPackages, listFilesWithSuffix, undocumented); no duplication introduced.
No runtime instability surface — change is a test-only Go file plus two documentation rows; product runtime byte-identical to pre-bug HEAD.
No secrets, no network, no PII; the guard only reads repo files. No /home/ paths in evidence (redacted). gofmt/go vet clean.

Wrote bug spec/design/scopes/report/uservalidation/state/scenario-manifest; added a DevOps-sweep evidence breadcrumb to the parent report.md. No parent planning-truth edit, so no parent recertification required.

completedScopes: Scope 1: Document the drifted package and add a fail-loud doc-freshness guard

modernization.diffEvidenceReason: DevOps stochastic-quality-sweep Round 1 leaves changes uncommitted for the parent batch-commit; diff-evidence enforcement runs at parent commit time against the committed diff.
